results.name: pridemate
results.description: Effects that put +1/+1 counters on something when you gain life.

results.name: primal (ffxiv)
results.description: The various entities in FFXIV that are that game's version of Summons

results.name: prismari college
results.description: The blue-red college in Strixhaven.

results.name: probe
results.description: Draw three cards, then discard two cards. Not to be confused with [compulsive research](compulsive-research) or [sift](sift).

results.name: processing
results.description: Removes a card of your opponent's from the exile zone for value or to deny them resources.

results.name: progress engine
results.description: The faction of Blue-aligned New Phyrexians

results.name: promotes to commander
results.description: Cards that make things that aren't commanders, commanders.

results.name: prone
results.description: Laying down or laying flat face down.

results.name: propellor hat
results.description: A hat with a propellor on top, often rainbow coloured. Mostly worn by kids and to demonstrate whimsy.

results.name: prosperity
results.description: A town in Thunder Junction.

results.name: prosthetics
results.description: Artificial implants that replace or augment body parts.

results.name: protection
results.description: Effects relating to protecting your permanents. (Not necessarily via the protection keyword.)

results.name: protects-all
results.description: Effects that give a form of protection to all permanents of a kind. (That may be hexproof, indestructible, protection, etc.)

results.name: protects-creature
results.description: Effects that can protect a creature, e.g. with protection,  hexproof, indestructible, etc.

results.name: provoke lite
results.description: "That creature must block this one this combat if able." It's like Provoke, but without the untapping. Also similar to [lure](lure) and [lure-limited](lure-limited).

results.name: pseudo-dethrone
results.description: Cards that give benefits if their owner is attacking the opponent/player with the highest life total

results.name: pseudo-equipment
results.description: Before Equipment arrived in Mirrodin, WotC experimented with a number of effects that granted boosts that stuck but could be transferred.

results.name: pseudo-fog
results.description: Effects that can protect you from an entire combat phase, similar to the card Fog, but using less conventional means than damage prevention.

results.name: pseudo-hexproof
results.description: Cards that do something very much like hexproof, but not hexproof.

results.name: pseudo-legendary
results.description: Cards that aren't typed 'legendary', yet still have rules that prevent multiple copies of them being in play simultaneously.

results.name: pseudo-leveler
results.description: Creatures with abilities that add incremental layers of growth.

results.name: pseudo-shroud
results.description: Prevents targeting from certain things from any player, including it's controller.

results.name: pterodactyl
results.description: Pterodactyls and other pterosaurs.

results.name: pteruges
results.description: A pleated skirt formed of straps of leather worn by Roman and Greek soldiers
